🌡️ Preparing Your UK Farm for Heatwaves: Complete 2026 Guide

Climate Reality: UK Heatwaves Are Increasing

2025 saw record temperatures across Britain. 2026 is forecast to bring more extreme heat events. This guide helps you prepare.

Region-Specific Heat Risks

### 🔥 South-East England (Highest Risk) - Risk level: Very High - Typical peaks: 35-40°C - Vulnerable: Horticulture, poultry, dairy - Key prep: Irrigation, shade structures ### 🌡️ Midlands & East Anglia (High Risk) - Risk level: High - Typical peaks: 32-38°C - Vulnerable: Arable, root crops - Key prep: Soil moisture, early harvesting ### 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Scotland & North (Moderate Risk) - Risk level: Moderate - Typical peaks: 28-32°C - Vulnerable: Livestock (not acclimated) - Key prep: Ventilation, water access

Water Management Strategies

### Immediate Actions (Do Now) 1. Check water storage – Clean tanks, repair leaks 2. Test pumps – Ensure backup power 3. Map water points – Ensure livestock access 4. Calculate needs: - Dairy cows: 100-150L/day each in heat - Beef cattle: 40-80L/day - Sheep: 4-8L/day - Pigs: 15-30L/day ### Medium-Term (This Month) - Install additional troughs - Create water redundancy (multiple sources) - Consider rainwater harvesting expansion - Check abstraction licenses ### Long-Term (Before Summer) - Reservoir expansion - Drip irrigation for high-value crops - Shade infrastructure

Livestock Cooling Solutions

### Shade Structures | Type | Cost | Effectiveness | Best For | |------|------|---------------|----------| | Natural tree belts | Low | High | Pastures | | Permanent sheds | £5,000-20,000 | Very High | Dairy yards | | Shade cloth | £500-2,000 | Medium | Temporary | | Umbrellas | £200-500 | Low | Individual animals | ### Active Cooling - Misting systems: £2,000-8,000 (very effective) - Fans: £100-500 each (barns/poultry) - Wallowing areas: £500-2,000 (pigs/cattle) ### Species-Specific Tips Dairy Cows: - Most vulnerable to heat stress - Need shade + ventilation + water - Consider night milking in heatwaves - Watch for panting, drooling, lethargy Sheep: - Shear before peak summer - Provide shade in afternoon - Check flies (heat increases problems) Pigs: - High risk (can't sweat) - Wallowing essential - Ventilated housing critical - Frozen treats help Poultry: - Highest mortality risk in heat - Max ventilation - Reduce stocking density - Provide electrolytes in water

Crop Protection

### Before Heatwave - Irrigate deeply 2-3 days before (not shallow) - Avoid spraying – droplets magnify heat - Check mulch – maintain 5cm+ organic layer - Harvest early if crops near ready ### During Heatwave - Morning irrigation only (5-9 AM) - Shade cloth for vegetables (£1-2/m²) - Mist systems for high-value crops - Stop fertilizer – increases water needs ### After Heatwave - Assess damage – photograph for insurance - Water gradually – don't flood shocked plants - Check for disease – heat stress = vulnerability - Plan improvements for next year

Emergency Response Checklist

### 24 Hours Before Forecast Heat - [ ] All water troughs full and flowing - [ ] Shade structures deployed - [ ] Emergency contacts list updated - [ ] Vet on standby notice - [ ] Check insurance coverage ### During Heatwave - [ ] Check animals every 2 hours - [ ] Monitor water consumption - [ ] Activate cooling systems - [ ] Document temperatures - [ ] Postpone non-essential work ### Post-Heatwave - [ ] Full health check all livestock - [ ] Inspect crops for damage - [ ] Review what worked/didn't - [ ] Update plans for next event - [ ] Claim insurance if applicable

Investment Priorities

High Priority (Do This Year): 1. Water redundancy (£500-2,000) 2. Shade cloth for vulnerable areas (£1,000-3,000) 3. Ventilation fans (£500-1,500) Medium Priority (Next 1-2 Years): 1. Permanent shade structures (£5,000-20,000) 2. Irrigation expansion (£3,000-10,000) 3. Misting systems (£2,000-8,000) Funding Available: - Farming Investment Fund (equipment) - SFI (water efficiency, tree planting) - Countryside Stewardship (capital items)

Get Help

- AHDB Weather Hub: ahdb.org.uk/weather - Met Office Heat Alerts: metoffice.gov.uk - Vet advice: Your local practice - NFU support: nfuonline.com *Prepare now. Heatwaves kill livestock and crops – but preparation saves both.* *Last updated: 26 February 2026*
